title: Level 327
wikitext:
{{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css?family=Electrolize'); /* Font */ html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: 'Electrolize'}} {{Transparent Theme 2}} {{Background|file=Lvl 327 v2.jpg}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:
Level 327: Halls Of Violence}} {{Warning|content= extreme gore, violence, torture and body horror. If you're not comfortable with these themes, please don't read this page. If you are, I hope you like it}} ‎ {{Class Ψ|bg-image=Level 327.jpg|class=Ψ|safety=Unsafe|security=Worsens With Time|entity=Aggression Hazards|hexagon=Ψ}} '''Level 327''' is the 328th level of the Backrooms. ==
Description == {{Blurimage|file=Lvl 327 v2.jpg|caption=The first and most well known photo of Level 327}}Level 327 is an infinite maze-like structure of orange hallways that resemble an industrial complex. Said halls are decorated with hanging lamps, the floor, walls and ceiling are dull and with an extremely scarce amount of decoration and the ceiling takes a round shape, with a height of around 4 meters. However, the ceiling patterns subtly vary in each hallway and sometimes repeat themselves; this creates a sensation of familiarity and even deja vu while walking by brand new hallways. With their long distances between bifurcations, one may feel like the ending is distant and there is always more to walk. Standing still feels wrong, leading to excessive walking, and stopping to relax for a moment is a rare occurrence on this level. '''Level 327''' is at a constant temperature of 16 ºC, but it creates a fake sensation that it fluctuates; wanderers will feel warm or cold at random moments. A theory claims that those temperature variations can indicate proximity towards nearby wanderers, but as of right now, it is nowhere near proven to be right. Staircases can rarely be found as one traverses throughout the level, meaning that there are many floors to it; as of right now, it is believed that the number is infinite. These staircases can take many different layouts, including traditional [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]] staircases (the most common variant, about 75% of the staircases feature this layout), spiral stairs (found in roughly 20% of staircases) and chaotic, irregular patterns found in particularly wide and dark rooms as runways (which are only around 5%). {{Blurimage|file=Lvl 327 normal staircase.jpg|caption=A staircase found in the level that belongs to the common variant}} Throughout the level, one might rarely find rooms with supplies and weapons. These have an aesthetic that greatly contrasts with the rest of the level, having a much lower ceiling than the standard hallways and slightly dirty concrete walls. They appear randomly throughout the level and are always behind a door that serves as their only way in; there hasn't been found a single supply room with more than one door. The supplies inside these rooms include glass water bottles, plasters, bandages, knives, daggers, swords, axes, katanas, scythes and, very rarely, shotguns, rifles and bullets. It is assumed that fire arms and bullets will never share a room, and bullets are compatible with every single fire arm in the level. Despite '''Level 327''''s general stiff aesthetic and the degraded state of the rooms themselves, the supplies are in perfect state and placed orderly throughout the room, as if they had been purposefully prepared somehow. {{Blurimage|file=Weird ahh 327 staircase.jpg|caption=One of the irregular runway staircases found in the level; a fake window can also be seen at the very bottom}} The level occasionally, albeit very rarely, features windows; however, they do not lead anywhere at all. These project a fake image of a bluish yet cloudy sky, and if broken, they will give the impression of pieces of glass falling down. The clouds outside do not move, or if they do, their movement is imperceptible. Prolonged staring tends to bring a peaceful feeling and an increasing conviction that jumping out will make one float away from the level. If a wanderer tries to jump out, a phenomenon with no logical explanation will force them back inside the room or hallway they were in. One of the strangest properties of '''Level 327''' is its audition, which can be described as anomalous, misleading and confusing. The level itself is completely silent, with the only audible sounds being those that wanderers make; the problem lies in the way it spreads. Although sounds reverb a highly notable amount, distant hearing is slightly poorer than in the Frontrooms or most Backrooms levels. Walking sounds hollow, but the echo does not move away, it stays close, and trying to move slowly and quietly will not make the steps sound softer, creating an uneasy sensation of never being truly alone even when nobody is nearby. According to several testimonials of survivors, as one stays longer and longer, it becomes increasingly difficult to tell who is making the steps or where they come from, even leading to wanderers confusing their very own steps. Echoes do not seem to follow a clear pattern; on some moments they arrive with a delay, other times they come from the wrong direction (most commonly from behind), and some sounds might even repeat themselves more than once with small variations per time, as if the environment could not decide when they should fade away. Even breathing is a common source of confusion and misleadings, as it is perceived as intense and overly close, and if one stays in '''Level 327''' for long enough, it even sounds out of phase. Breathing that does not sound "own" results particularly disturbing; it is easily noticeable but the direction where it comes from is unclear. Because of this, hearing heavy breathing isn't necessarily a sign that other wanderer is close, as there is a chance that it might actually be one's own breathing distorted by the level's odd auditive properties. Another enigmatic property is that every area of the level always seems to be in perfect state, which seems contradictory given the destruction caused on the level. The most widely accepted theory is that the level can somehow regenerate its own areas when no wanderers are nearby. A damaged wall will later look clean and untoched, if there is blood it will eventually vanish, broken doors will return back to place, broken lights will eventually fix themselves, etc. The level always seems perfect, yet nobody has seen it regenerate. Despite that, the fact that damaged areas are often found restored when revisited has led to the theory that an area will reset whenever nobody is nearby. Aside from the audition and the likely regeneration properties, Level 327 lacks any special physical features. However, it is known for a dangerous psychological hazard that happens to be entirely exclusive to it, which is the reason why this level should be avoided as much as possible. ==
The 327 Effect == The biggest hazard of '''Level 327''' is '''The 327 Effect''', a psychological effect unique in The Backrooms that triggers a permanent state of anger, thirst of destruction and sadistic behavior in wanderers, reason why '''Level 327''' got its nickname. This can make the level considerably dangerous despite the absence of entities, since as soon as two wanderers encounter, they will fight each other to death. Several found notes helped reveal that The 327 Effect causes wanderers to believe they're the only ones who deserve to escape The Backrooms, and that everyone else deserves suffering and a painful death even if they haven't done anything wrong. '''The 327 Effect''' is divided in 15 stages, each with a random duration that can range between 9 and 15 hours, and begins to affect one as soon as they arrive on '''Level 327'''. ===
Stage 1: "Baseline" === ====
''Stage 1 does not sow fear. It sows trust.'' ==== Upon entry to '''Level 327''', wanderers will have a completely unaltered mindset. They'll be able to think clearly, their morality will be intact and there's no need to hurt others. Upon encounter with other wanderers in later stages, one may try to get away from them instead of fighting back, and cooperation between different wanderers at this stage is possible. Thoughts are clear and practical, the level is perceived as stable and "just one more challenge to face", the environment is perceived just like it is and interaction between wanderers is normal, with no underlying cynicism and even allowing for humor. The body is slightly alert but there is nothing out of the ordinary. ''This stage is harmless, but that's what makes '''The 327 Effect''' much more horrifying as it evolves.'' ===
Stage 2: "Cognitive Noise" === ====
''Stage 2 does not create tension. It generates a question that nobody tries to answer.'' ==== At this point, the wanderer is still cooperative and perfectly functional; however, their mind begins to have the first few psychological glitches. They'll begin to develop subtle but consistent intrusive thoughts and have a mild feeling that something's wrong or odd. Cooperation is still possible, the level is seen as slightly uncomfortable but far from a threat and wanderers can think clearly. The body becomes a little bit tenser than before, but it is a minimal and close to invisible change. ''This stage normalizes dissonance, training the mind to reinterpretate reality and setting the mood for the next step down.'' ===
Stage 3: "Social Friction" === ====
''Stage 3 does not create enemies. It makes others begin to look like ones.'' ==== This is the point where the wanderer begins to become irritable and distrustful of others. One will still perceive themselves as reasonable, but they no longer see good intentions in others and they will start getting upset over small things, misinterpretating neutral gestures as provocative, seeing hidden intentions behind every action and anticipating conflict. The wanderer's expression becomes significantly more serious, smiling is close to impossible, the body is tense and nearby stimuli create hostile reactions. Because of this, cooperation becomes absolutely impossible at this stage, even for the most normally agreeable wanderers. ''Explicit violence has not occurred yet, but there is a change of mind that will shortly lead to it.'' ===
Stage 4: "Justification Loop" === ====
''Stage 4 does not teach to attack. It teaches to justify violence.'' ==== The wanderer will begin having thoughts that justify or encourage violence against others. Aggression starts being deemed reasonable, and although harm isn't the end goal, it is perceived as a resource that shall be used if need be. There is no intention to kill yet, just prevention, and therefore intimidation or smaller acts of violence like hitting or pushing in self-defense might take place. Survivors of the level claim to remember having thoughts like "I'm tense about my survival because anything could be a threat" or "this wanderer could potentially harm me". This stage doesn't teach how to attack yet, it teaches how to justify violence. ''Once violence is accepted as a valid option, the next decision will be easy and clear.'' ===
Stage 5: "First Breach" === ====
''Stage 5 does not make you lose mercy. It makes you decide it is not necessary.'' ==== When the wanderer reaches this stage, violence stops being reactive in order to become deliberate, because from now on, the wanderer will start actively fighting others to death. The wanderer knows they're going to kill; they have time to stop and change their mind but they still decide to murder. No longer will there be intimidation or warnings, all those thoughts are replaced by a new, simple and primal one: "kill or be killed". Murder is seen as inevitable and preventive, and other wanderers are seen as potential threats that must be eliminated; the moral line does no longer exist. ''The '''First Breach''' isn't just an action, but an internal rewriting of identity, values and decisions, and from now on, the wanderer does not live with the same mentality.'' ===
Stage 6: "Bloodlust Buildup" === ====
''Stage 6 does not make killing pleasant. It makes living without killing insufferable.'' ==== Once here, the wanderer not only kills for survival, but starts finding solace in murder. Violence alleviates tension and lowers anxiety, the body feels "clearer" and more stable after killing and an association between causing harm and bodily stability is born, as if one's own body rewarded aggression... and because of that, the wanderer unconsciously starts searching for that reward. Right after a kill, one will notice that the level "feels less threatening", silence becomes lighter, walking is easier and the heartbeat stabilizes. Killing is now interpreted as the only way to survive the level and living without killing becomes unbearable, creating a frightening addiction to violence. ''Conditioning is done, and from now on, violence just needs a minimal excuse, creating an addiction to it and eventually leading to the next stage: searching for it.'' ===
Stage 7: "Target Fixation" === ====
''Stage 7 does not turn you into a killer. It turns you into a hunter.'' ==== No longer will the wanderer avoid encounters with others once they reach this stage; instead, they will actively look for people to fight and kill. Waiting for an encounter is no longer an option, now it's all about trying to cause it, and if they sense someone is nearby, they will start searching for their next victim like a lion pursuing gazelles. One will start identifying potential routes of other wanderers and anticipating their movements in order to find, fight and kill them. The environment becomes a hunting map as attention becomes selective and predatory, and killing is no longer the response to an encounter, but an objective to which every action is aimed at. ''Eventually, this sets the tone for something worse: when searching is no longer enough and a much darker motivation arises.'' ===
Stage 8: "Moral Inversion" === ====
''Stage 8 does not remove morality. It decides that everyone deserves punishment.'' ==== Ethics begin to twist as the wanderer's hunger of violence keeps growing bigger. A belief that everyone deserves to die arises, and the wanderer begins to feel entitled to escape the level and The Backrooms as a whole while nobody else should. Others stop being individuals and start feeling like obstacles that should not be there and are not deserving of empathy, compassion or mercy. In addition, surviving encounters further feeds the belief that the wanderer is meant to make it while the others are not. The mind rewrites basic values: cooperation becomes weakness and others' good becomes a threat. ''And when someone believes they're the only ones who deserve to survive, the next step is wondering: "why should I limit myself to just people?"'' ===
Stage 9: "Resource Hostility" === ====
''Stage 9 does not only make you kill. It makes you ensure others' survival is impossible.'' ==== Blinded by the belief that they're the only one who deserves to escape the level, the wanderer will now start to sabotage the material resources that the level offers. The mindset is that everything that grants help to other wanderers is a threat, and hence shall be damaged. If they don't take something, they'll destroy it in order to ensure that nobody else can use it to their advantage, because every usable object becomes something that could be used against the wanderer later. ''After destroying both people and resources, the next step is the curiosity on the process, leading to '''Stage 10'''.'' ===
Stage 10: "Sadistic Drift" === ====
''Stage 10 is not when you become cruel. It is the point where you discover cruelty pleases you.'' ==== Violence is no longer just functional, and others' pain starts to matter by itself, leading the wanderer to prolong the harm, observe it and interact with it. The wanderer will begin to torture their victims, kill them slowly and watch them suffer during their last moments. The wanderer starts becoming interested in testing how long others can last and seeing expressions of pain in their faces, often testing various methods of harm such as choking, applying pressure or stabbing in multiple locations out of curiosity, making the act as important as the outcome. Watching the other dreadfully pass away brings the wanderer pleasure. ''One step further and the wanderer asks themselves: "what if I destroy this whole place?"'' ===
Stage 11: "Environmental Aggression" === ====
''Stage 11 does not only want to win. It longs for the environment's demise.'' ==== Hate expands as one not only fights fellow wanderers trapped in the level, but the whole level, the entire reality, and the sadism is directed towards both people and material things. Alongside the curiosity on others' suffering from '''Stage 10''', they also enjoy seeing the destruction they cause on the level, and they will break objects and might start hitting walls or attempting to tear doors or lamps down. Now everything is to be attacked as the wanderer watches how everything around them crumbles down. ''After attacking the entire environment, one will reach an inevitable point: when violence stops being targeted and becomes hatred towards reality as a whole.'' ===
Stage 12: "Existential Rage" === ====
''Stage 12 does not only destroy the world. It wishes it never existed in the first place.'' ==== This is pure hatred. The wanderer develops hateful sadistic thoughts towards every single thing they have ever come across, including The Frontrooms, and a belief that the entire reality is against them. Nostalgic childhood memories that used to create comfort now generate wrath, happy moments are reinterpreted as lies and the wanderer now sees their loved ones as backstabbers, making the past stop being a refuge and become proof that everything was always a plan against the wanderer. One's whole identity is now sustained only by hatred, under a "me vs reality" mindset. ''After hating the world, the past and The Frontrooms, there's only one next level: the end of a coherent identity.'' ===
Stage 13: "Identity Collapse" === ====
''Stage 13 is not where you lose who you are. It is when it doesn't even matter.'' ==== The wanderer is no longer a person that hates, they're hatred with a body. Hate grows so strong in the wanderer that they start forgetting things about themselves, their past and what led them to what they are right now, or they believe it does not even matter. Everything is an obstacle, and in order to survive and escape, the wanderer will destroy everything. They define themselves only by their survival instinct, their hate, their distrust and the harm they cause, they justify they actions as "the only way to survive in an extremely dangerous reality" and nothing else matters anymore. ''But even at such levels of violence, it's still possible to take things further, and at one point, escaping '''The Backrooms''' will no longer be important and survival will become just a tool, leading to '''Stage 14'''.'' ===
Stage 14: "Terminal Obsession" === ====
''Stage 14 does not want to survive. It wants to be the sole survivor.'' ==== A single idea invades the wanderer's head: destroying everything. Extreme violence is present, now without an objective in particular other than just harm and destroy. Unlike all previous stages where there was still a certain desire to escape, here that is completely gone, the wanderer's only goal is to leave the level as broken as they could possibly imagine. While they still care about their survival, they only see it as a tool to reach their ultimate goal; if they survive they're able to keep destroying. All one cares about now is the destructive imprint they leave behind. ''At one point, the body won't be able to take it anymore, and aggression will be directed not only outwardly, but also inwardly, taking the final step.'' ===
Stage 15: "Self Destruction Abyss" === ====
''Stage 15 does not kill you. It allows you to do it yourself.'' ==== At this final, horrible phase, one will start to self-harm out of rage. Under a state of violent catatonia, the wanderer's aggression keeps being directed towards everything in the level, but unlike Stage 14, now that everything includes the wanderer themselves. It is not conscious suicide, neither is it regret, it is something worse: violence no longer distinguishes between the inside and the outside. The self is now seen as another thing that exists, and hence shall be destroyed; there is no difference between punching a wall, fighting another wanderer and attacking oneself. Death will inevitably occur at this stage, whether directly or indirectly. ==
Entities == '''Level 327''' is completely devoid of entities. ''It turns YOU into a monster.'' ==
Found Notes ==

Found Note
''' This audio was recorded on October 29, 2011. It is a partly corrupted conversation between three wanderers (two males and a female) aggressively arguing until eventually the female (labeled here as W2) pulls out a shotgun and kills the two males. W2 eventually managed to escape the level and works at an outpost now, however she remembers this as her "worst experience ever" and she herself uploaded the log to "warn others about what '''Level 327''' does to one's behavior". ----
'''''W1''': I'm telling you we shouldn't go all the way up this staircase.'' '''''W2''': And where do you want to go then?'' '''''W1''': Anywhere but all the way to the top.'' '''''W3''': That makes no sense.'' '''''W1''': Neither does questioning what I say.'' '''''W2''': I'm thinking of it.'' '''''W1''': It doesn't look like you're thinking at all.'' ''[CORRUPTED AUDIO]'' '''''W3''': You two should relax and stop arguing over meaningless bullshit.'' '''''W2''': I'm already relaxed.'' '''''W1''': You're not.'' '''''W2''': And you think YOU are chill right now?'' ''[Silence]'' ''[CORRUPTED AUDIO]'' '''''W3''': Seriously, I don't like what you're doing.'' '''''W1''': WHAT THE FUCK AM I DOING?'' '''''W3''': The way you're looking at me.'' '''''W1''': EXCUSE ME?'' '''''W3''': What the fuck do I have for you to keep staring me for HALF AN HOUR STRAIGHT?'' '''''W1''': I'm just looking at the hallway and the lamps.'' '''''W2''': DON'T FUCKING LIE TO US'' '''''W1''': YOU'RE GOING TO TELL ME WHERE I AM LOOKING AT OR WHAT ON EARTH IS WRONG WITH YOU?'' '''''W3''': I'M ONLY SAYING THAT-'' '''''W1''': YOU'RE SAYING WHAT, HUH?'' ''[Tense Silence]'' ''[CORRUPTED AUDIO]'' '''''W2''': We're not going anywhere if both of you keep acting like that.'' '''''W1''': Then stop giving orders.'' '''''W2''': I'm not giving orders!'' '''''W1''': You keep talking as if you knew everything, and no, in reality you have NO IDEA what you're doing.'' '''''W3''': Dude, stop.'' '''''W1''': No, SHE is the one that has to stop.'' '''''W2''': I HAVEN'T DONE ANYTHING! WHAT THE FUCK ARE YOU TALKING ABOUT?'' '''''W1''': See? THAT is the problem!'' ''[Dry Blow]'' '''''W3''': Hey! HEY!'' '''''W1''': You pushed me first!'' '''''W3''': Because you were getting too close!'' '''''W1''': Can't I even talk to you properly?'' '''''W3''': SHUT UP!'' ''[CORRUPTED AUDIO]'' '''''W1''': Ok ok ok ok we've had enough, stop fighting.'' '''''W2''': Get the fuck away!'' '''''W3''': No, YOU get away!'' '''''W1''': I SAID STOP!'' ''[Loud Blow]'' '''''W2''': DON'T FUCKING TOUCH ME!'' '''''W3''': I didn't touch you!'' '''''W2''': You were trying to do it!'' '''''W3''': No, I wasn't!'' '''''W2''': Of course you were, stop lying!'' ''[CORRUPTED AUDIO]'' '''''W3''': Put that gun down.'' '''''W2''': No.'' '''''W3''': There's no need to do this.'' '''''W2''': There is.'' '''''W1''': Are you serious?'' '''''W2''': Why are you looking at me like that?'' '''''W3''': Nobody is looking at you weirdly.'' '''''W2''': Yes, both of you are.'' '''''W1''': You're paranoid.'' '''''W2''': No.'' '''''W1''': Yes.'' '''''W2''': Step back.'' '''''W1''': Or else?'' '''''W2''': Or else I'll shoot.'' '''''W3''': Girl...'' '''''W1''': You're not shooting.'' ''[Shot]'' ''[Long & Tense Silence]'' '''''W3''': WHAT THE FUCK HAVE YOU DONE?'' '''''W2''': HE WAS TRYING TO ATTACK ME, I HAD TO DEFEND MYSELF!'' '''''W3''': NO!'' '''''W2''': YES HE WAS!'' '''''W3''': Stay away from me!'' '''''W2''': We'll have to move him sooner or later.'' '''''W3''': Stay away.'' '''''W2''': If we leave him here somebody will-'' '''''W3''': STAY THE FUCK AWAY FROM ME!'' '''''W2''': Calm down, let's move the body.'' '''''W3''': GET AWAY!'' ''[Blow & Tussle]'' '''''W2''': STOP!'' '''''W3''': DON'T YOU DARE!'' ''[Shot]''

Found Note
''' This note was recorded and uploaded to the M.E.G. database on July 1, 2016, and it is considered one of the most horrifying pieces of footage ever recorded in The Backrooms because of its extreme gore. It is a video of two male wanderers who cross each other, they fight but the one here labeled as W2 is significantly stronger and easily beats W1. After that, however, W2 is not done, and he cuts W1 through many parts of his body before brutally decapitating him. ----
'''''W1''': I heard you from the hallway, get out. I know where you are.'' '''''W2:''' I also know you're here.'' '''''W1''': Then get out.'' '''''W2''': I already know you were here either way, you didn't even have to make noise.'' ''[Both wanderers approach each other from both sides]'' '''''W1''': If you're going to do it, do it now.'' '''''W2''': That depends on you.'' '''''W1''': Perfect.'' ''[The two wanderers physically fight each other]'' '''''W2''': Stop.'' '''''W1''': Let me go!'' '''''W2''': Stop.'' '''''W1''': Let me-'' ''['''W1''' falls to the floor]'' '''''W2''': That's it... don't move'' '''''W1''': Fuck you.'' '''''W2''': You thought this would end quickly, right?'' '''''W1''': Come on, keep fighting and it will.'' '''''W2''': Fight? First of all, you won't last much anyways, and second, do you really think I'm done?'' '''''W1''': What?'' '''''W2''': This is where the worst part begins.'' '''''W1''': Go fuck yourself.'' '''''W2''': No, this is the point when you'll ACTUALLY suffer.'' ''['''W2''' pulls a knife out and stabs '''W1''' in the heart]'' '''''W1''': Don't touch me, idiot.'' '''''W2''': Don't move.'' '''''W1''': Are you going to kill me or what?'' '''''W2''': If I wanted to kill you quickly, you'd be dead by now.'' '''''W1''': STOP YOU FUCKING PIECE OF SHIT!'' ''['''W2''' keeps making deep cuts through '''W1''''s skin with his knife]'' '''''W1''': WHAT ARE YOU DOING YOU ASSHOLE?'' '''''W2''': Nothing I've done for the first time here.'' '''''W1''': For fuck's sake, stop.'' '''''W2''': Calm down.'' ''['''W1''' starts screaming in agonizing pain]'' '''''W1''': STOP!'' '''''W2''': Yeah... this looks good...'' '''''W1:''' WHY ARE YOU DOING THIS TO ME?'' '''''W2''': Just look how you're changing.'' ''['''W1''' loses his voice]'' '''''W1''': Just... stop... now... ggghhh...'' '''''W2''': There's still a little bit more left.'' '''''W1''': Ggghhh... ggghhh... you're a... fucking... idiot...'' ''[After a couple seconds of silence, '''W2''' brutally decapitates '''W1''']''

Found Note
'''This note's author or writing date remain unknown, but it's a wanderer insulting every facet or reality, showing clear Stage 12 signs[[File:Lvl 327 handwritten.JPEG|thumb|957x957px|center]] ----
'''> OPEN FOUND NOTE 4'''
'''> CLOSE FOUND NOTE 4'''
'''
Found Note
''' This audio was published on August 5, 2022 by a wanderer who miraculously escaped the level by accident on Stage 15, being the only documented case of this occurrence. This example is used to understand better the wanderer's thought process. ----
''(irregular breathing)'' ''Alright... I decided to speak about this... just to let you know of the dangers those orange hallways await. I need to say it out loud... to keep people away from that nightmare of a level.'' ''When I entered that level... what number is it even? 316? 319? 327? 345? something like that. Anyways... when I entered it with [REDACTED], nothing was wrong... it was just hallways, silence and us two walking in peace. But slowly... tension started rising. At first it was that we argued over the smallest things... and we didn't trust each other... then it escalated and... I... even hit him a few times... but it wasn't even the worst. Eventually... (sobbing) I... killed... him. (breaks down crying).'' ''After like my... fourth victim I think... I felt like I was in peace... and harmony... but slowly my thoughts started blurring again... and the same thing happened with my... next... victims... like I felt some awkward stability... or comfort... after each murder... I'd start attempting to hunt them down... stepping on food and... even... hiding bullets in it... so that... they... were... deadly traps.'' ''The more time I was there... the more unreasonably violent I was getting... I remember... dismembering a tall and muscular man alive... and his screams of... agonizing pain... fear... and resentment... I think his last words were... "you'll pay for this, this won't end like this"... I also remember... tearing down a door... and smashing a middle-aged blonde woman with it... in her head... dozens of times... with her yelling at me... what was... wrong with me... and later... I even said I'd do the same... to my family... calling them snakes... wishing them to be... tortured forever... and die a painful death... I'm so sorry for that... I guess The Backrooms... make you lose your mind... and beyond that point, I can't even... remember what I did clearly.'' ''From trust, to paranoia, to violence, to strategy, to sadism, to hatred, to collapse, and now, remorse. Looking back, the experience was horrifying. I might be free now but... the shit I did in the level... it was traumatic. I still can't believe... I actually... did the stuff I did... and the remorse I feel... for these horrible, inhuman actions I committed... will haunt me forever. May all my victims... rest in peace... they didn't deserve this fate. Avoid this level... this nightmare... at all costs. For your own good.''
==
Entrances & Exits == ===
Entrances === * Successfully killing a Snare in [[Level 410]] will take one here; * Attempting to intentionally drown in [[Level 642]] will lead here; * Noclipping through a boiler in [[Level 297]] will lead one here; * If one drills a hole in any of [[Level 570]]'s walls, this level will be seen beyond. ===
Exits === * Falling down a staircase leads to a [[Level 330]] pool; however, one will forget that this way out exists as soon as they set foot in '''Level 327''' * There is an extremely rare golden knife with a lapis lazuli grip in a supply room. Managing to carry that golden knife without using it for 12 hours straight will send one to [[Level 866]]. * Running 250 meters under half a minute has a chance of creating a portal in the floor right in front of you, entering it will take you to [[Level 444]] * A legend states that apologizing for the harm caused while inside the level can lead one to [[Level 155]], [[Level 490]] or [[Level 666]]; however, such ocurrence is realistically impossible because of the level's nature [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Dangerous]] [[Category:Mental Hazards]]
